story.iWant: automatic snapshot creation every 100 events for aggregates
story.soThat: aggregate loading performance remains fast even with long event histories

tasks.task: Verify snapshot_entry table from V001 migration
Write performance test: 1000 events → load time
Commit: "Add automatic snapshot creation every 100 events"

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Integration test creates aggregate with 250+ events and verifies snapshots created
Aggregate loading test validates snapshot usage (loads from snapshot, not full history)
Performance improvement measured and documented (>10x faster for 1000+ events)

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Snapshot strategy (every 100 events, configurable). Performance improvement >10x faster for 1000+ events.

doc.title: Decision Architecture
doc.snippet: Snapshot strategy (every 100 events, configurable). Jackson serialization for snapshot payload. Performance target: >10x improvement for aggregates with 1000+ events.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: SnapshotTriggerDefinition (every 100 events). Jackson serialization configured. snapshot_entry schema validated. Performance >10x improvement measured.

dependency.name: jackson-module-kotlin
dependency.usage: Jackson Kotlin module for snapshot serialization

constraints.constraint: Jackson serialization for snapshot payload (no custom serializers)
Snapshot loading must be transparent (aggregate doesn't know about snapshots)

interface.name: SnapshotTriggerDefinition
interface.purpose: Defines when snapshots should be created

pattern.name: Snapshot Configuration Pattern

standards.performance: Snapshot loading >10x faster than full event replay

ideas.idea: Test SnapshotTriggerDefinition bean is configured with threshold 100
Test Jackson serializer is configured for snapshot payload
Test snapshot_entry table exists with correct schema (from V001 migration)
Integration test: create aggregate, apply 250 events, verify 2 snapshots created (at 100, 200)
Integration test: verify snapshot sequence numbers are correct (100, 200)
Integration test: load aggregate, verify it uses snapshot (not full replay)
Integration test: verify aggregate state matches after snapshot load
Performance test: measure aggregate loading time with 1000 events (without snapshot)
Performance test: measure aggregate loading time with 1000 events (with snapshot at 1000)
Performance test: assert snapshot loading is >10x faster
